flashcards.definition: V₁/T₁ = V₂/T₂; at constant pressure, volume is directly proportional to temperature (K).
As temperature increases, volume increases (V ↑ when T ↑).
0 K (−273.15°C) — the theoretical temperature where gas volume would be zero.
The absolute temperature scale; K = °C + 273.15. Must use Kelvin in gas laws.
Hot air balloons rise because heated air expands, becoming less dense.
